analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$78,281 on May 3 consolidating below $80K resistance (+1.89% 24h). Broke out of multi-month descending channel. Down ~38% from Oct 2025 ATH of $126K. Market cap ~$2.68T (+0.1% 24h — very flat).
sentiment_key_signals: BTC consolidating at $78,281 — range-bound below $80K psychological resistance
April spot BTC ETF monthly inflows: strongest since October 2025; May 1 single-day $629.8M (BlackRock IBIT $284.4M, Fidelity FBTC $213.4M)
Ethereum ETFs returned to inflows May 1: $101.2M net
Iran submitted updated peace proposal to Pakistan mediators May 1 — positive geopolitical development
US crude futures fell ~5% on Iran peace signal — risk-on implications
FOMC held rates 3.5-3.75% April 29; next meeting June 16-17
Fed Chair Powell exits May 15; Kevin Warsh replacing — transition uncertainty
Goldman Sachs SEC filing for Bitcoin Premium Income ETF (April 15)
Large-cap crypto range-bound; selective altcoin gains only — no broad breakout confirmed
Retail sentiment in extreme fear; whale wallets still accumulating
Ark Invest: BTC market cap to $16T by 2030 — bullish long-term outlook
analysis.macro_backdrop: Weekend May 3-4 — no major scheduled macro releases. FOMC held at 3.5-3.75% April 29. CPI ~3.3% YoY. Next key events: ISM Services May 5, NFP April data May 8, CPI/PPI ~May 12-13. Iran peace proposal is key near-term catalyst — if confirmed, risk-on rally likely.
analysis.regime_rationale: Neutral (0.50): BTC broke descending channel but is consolidating below critical $80K resistance on weekend low liquidity. Strong ETF inflows support bullish case, but large-caps are range-bound and retail fear is elevated. Iran peace proposal is a positive catalyst but not yet confirmed. Waiting for $80K breakout confirmation before upgrading to bullish. Macro filter active at 0.50 threshold.
